metadata.title: Debug Factorial Experiment — GCP Cloud Run with Claude Monitoring
metadata.prerequisite-branch: fix/pre-debug-qa-verification (must be merged to main first)
metadata.priority: P0 — validates entire pipeline before $65 production run
metadata.estimated-cost: ~$6-10 on GCP L4 spot (24 trainable + 2 zero-shot, 2 epochs each)

architecture.description:
Two-layer execution model:
Layer 1 (Deterministic): scripts/run_factorial.sh - Pure sky jobs launch calls in a loop - Reads configs/experiment/debug_factorial.yaml - Launches 24 trainable + 2 zero-shot = 26 conditions on GCP L4 - ANY researcher can run this without Claude Code - NO claude -p, NO screen, NO nohup, NO pipe chains
Layer 2 (Monitoring): Claude Code with Ralph Loop + TDD Skills - Monitors SkyPilot job status via `sky jobs queue` - Watches for failures, OOM, NaN, timeouts - On failure: diagnoses root cause, plans fix with reviewer agents - On success: verifies MLflow artifacts, metrics, params are complete - After all training: triggers post-training → analysis → biostatistics → deploy - If code changes needed: implements via self-learning TDD skill
CRITICAL SEPARATION: - run_factorial.sh is the PRODUCT (deterministic, reproducible, LLM-free) - Claude monitoring is the DEVELOPER TOOL (catches bugs, plans fixes) - The production run uses ONLY run_factorial.sh (no Claude needed)
architecture.banned: - claude -p wrapping run_factorial.sh (metalearning 2026-03-09, 2026-03-16) - screen -dmS for Claude sessions - nohup for any subprocess - --output-format stream-json | jq pipe chains - ANY bash wrapper around claude -p

prerequisites.prereq: make test-prod passes on main (0 failures)

GCP infrastructure verified: - Pulumi stack healthy: pulumi stack output - GAR image pushed: docker manifest inspect europe-north1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest - DVC data on GCS: dvc status -r gcs - SkyPilot GCP check: sky check gcp
.env configured with: - HF_TOKEN (SAM3 weights) - GCP credentials (Application Default Credentials) - MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI (local mlruns/ or Cloud Run URL)
Docker base image rebuilt with latest code: make build-base-gpu and make push-gar
DeepVess external test data downloaded and DVC-versioned: data/external/deepvess/ exists with image/label pairs

phase.name: Pre-Flight Verification
phase.description: Verify ALL prerequisites before spending cloud credits

tasks.task: Run: sky check gcp → verify GCP access
Run: docker manifest inspect GAR image → verify image exists
Run: pulumi stack output → verify GCP resources
Run: make test-staging → verify code is clean
Verify .env has all required variables
Verify debug_factorial.yaml parses correctly: python3 -c "import yaml; print(yaml.safe_load(open('configs/experiment/debug_factorial.yaml')))"

phase.gate: ALL tasks must pass. If ANY fails, STOP and fix before proceeding.

phase.name: Launch Training Conditions
phase.description: Execute run_factorial.sh for all 26 debug conditions

tasks.task: Launch: ./scripts/run_factorial.sh configs/experiment/debug_factorial.yaml
Ralph Loop monitors: sky jobs queue (every 2 minutes) Watch for: FAILED, CANCELLED, FAILED_SETUP states On failure: capture sky jobs logs JOB_ID and diagnose
Track progress: N/26 conditions completed Expected: ~5-10 min per condition on L4 (2 epochs, half data)

monitoring.check: sky jobs queue — count RUNNING, SUCCEEDED, FAILED
sky jobs logs LATEST_JOB — check for NaN, OOM, errors
monitoring.on-failure: 1. Capture full job log: sky jobs logs JOB_ID 2. Categorize: OOM / NaN / config error / infrastructure / transient 3. If transient (spot preemption): SkyPilot auto-recovers, no action 4. If OOM: check model VRAM profile, adjust patch_size or batch_size 5. If NaN: check BF16/FP16 dtype, loss function, learning rate 6. If config error: fix config, rebuild Docker, re-launch failed conditions 7. Plan fix with reviewer agents before implementing

phase.gate: All 26 conditions SUCCEEDED. If any FAILED after retry, diagnose and fix.

phase.name: Verify Training Artifacts
phase.description: Check that MLflow contains ALL expected data from training

tasks.task: Sync mlruns from GCP: gsutil rsync or cloud MLflow UI

For each of 24 trainable conditions, verify: - MLflow run exists with status=FINISHED - Params logged: model/family, train/loss_name, train/max_epochs, with_aux_calib - Metrics logged: train/loss, val/loss, val/dice (per epoch) - Metrics logged: grad/norm_mean, prof/train_seconds, vram/peak_mb - Artifacts: config/resolved_config.yaml, checkpoints/best_*.pth - Tags: flow_name, loss_function, fold_id, config_fingerprint
For each of 2 zero-shot conditions, verify: - SAM3 Vanilla: eval metrics on MiniVess fold-0 - VesselFM: test/deepvess/* metrics on DeepVess
Verify aux_calib factor actually differs: - Compare loss values for with_aux_calib=true vs false (same model+loss) - If identical → aux_calib is still dead code somewhere

phase.gate: ALL artifacts present for ALL conditions. No empty/missing values.

phase.name: Post-Training Flow
phase.description: Run post-training plugins on all training checkpoints

tasks.task: Launch post-training flow via Docker or SkyPilot: - SWA on all 24 trainable checkpoints - Calibration (if calibration_data available) - Verify post/{plugin}/{metric} logged to MLflow

phase.gate: Post-training artifacts exist for all applicable conditions.

phase.name: Analysis (Evals) Flow
phase.description: Evaluate all models on MiniVess + DeepVess external test

tasks.task: Run analysis flow: - Ensemble building from training + post-training checkpoints - Evaluation on MiniVess validation fold-0 (eval/ prefix metrics) - Evaluation on DeepVess external test (test/deepvess/ prefix metrics) - Champion model selection and tagging - Comparison table generation (markdown + LaTeX)

Verify: - eval/{model}/{subset}/{metric} metrics exist for all models - test/deepvess/{metric} metrics exist for all models - Champion tags written to MLflow - Comparison table includes both trainval and test columns

phase.gate: All evaluation metrics present. Champion models tagged.

phase.name: Biostatistics Flow
phase.description: Compute ALL statistical analyses for trainval AND test splits

tasks.task: Run biostatistics flow: - DuckDB materialization of all MLflow runs - split=trainval: ANOVA, pairwise, effect sizes, specification curve - split=test: same analyses on test/deepvess/* metrics - Figures: specification curve, interaction plot, CD diagram, box plots - Tables: ANOVA summary, rank discrepancy, pairwise comparisons

Verify: - DuckDB has both eval_metrics and test_metrics tables populated - Biostatistics output has both trainval and test splits - All expected figures generated (PNG/PDF) - All expected tables generated (CSV/LaTeX) - Per-volume metrics available for both splits

phase.gate: Complete statistical output for both splits. All figures render.

phase.name: Deploy Flow
phase.description: Test champion model deployment chain (local Docker, no cloud deploy)

tasks.task: Run deploy flow: - Champion discovery from MLflow tags - ONNX export (opset 17) - BentoML model store import - Deployment artifact generation (bentofile, docker-compose, README) - Model promotion (DEVELOPMENT → STAGING)
Verify: - ONNX model files exist and are valid - BentoML model store has imported models - bentofile.yaml generated correctly - Promotion audit trail logged

phase.gate: Full deploy pipeline completes without errors.

phase.name: Debug Run Report
phase.description: Summarize findings and plan production run

tasks.task: Generate debug run report: - Total cost (from SkyPilot job logs) - Wall-clock time per condition - Any failures encountered and how they were fixed - MLflow artifact completeness checklist - Biostatistics output quality assessment - Production run cost estimate (extrapolate from debug timing)
If ANY issues found: - Create GitHub issues with root cause - Fix in the debug/gcp-factorial-run branch - Re-run affected conditions - Verify fix resolved the issue

Production readiness decision: - All 26 debug conditions succeeded? → READY - All MLflow artifacts complete? → READY - Biostatistics produces valid output? → READY - Deploy flow works end-to-end? → READY - If ALL READY → authorize production run

phase.gate: Debug run report approved. Production run authorized.

production-run.description:
After debug run succeeds, the production run uses the SAME script: ./scripts/run_factorial.sh configs/hpo/paper_factorial.yaml
72 trainable + 6 zero-shot = 78 conditions, 50 epochs, full data, 3 folds. Estimated cost: ~$65 on GCP L4 spot. Estimated time: ~7-10 hours GPU (with spot preemption recovery).
The production run can optionally be monitored by Ralph Loop, but it MUST be runnable without any Claude Code involvement.

skill.name: ralph-loop
skill.role: Infrastructure monitoring during cloud execution. Polls sky jobs queue, detects failures, captures logs.

skill.name: self-learning-iterative-coder
skill.role: If code bugs are found during the debug run, implements fixes via TDD loop.
